title: Apple Sues Israeli Spyware Maker, Seeking to Block Its Access to iPhones
content: Apple accused NSO Group, the Israeli surveillance company, of “flagrant” violations of its software, as well as federal and state laws.
en_title: Apple Sues Israeli Spyware Maker, Seeking to Block Its Access to iPhones
en_content: Apple accused NSO Group, the Israeli surveillance company, of “flagrant” violations of its software, as well as federal and state laws.

title: As Virus Cases Rise in Europe, an Economic Toll Returns
content: A series of restrictions, including a lockdown in Austria, is expected to put a brake on economic growth.

more_info.byline: By Patricia Cohen and Melissa Eddy

en_title: As Virus Cases Rise in Europe, an Economic Toll Returns
en_content: A series of restrictions, including a lockdown in Austria, is expected to put a brake on economic growth.

title: Biden’s Low-Key Media Strategy Draws Allies’ Concern
content: So far in his tenure, the president has given far fewer one-on-one interviews than his two predecessors. Some Democrats are asking if he could be making better use of his White House pulpit.
en_title: Biden’s Low-Key Media Strategy Draws Allies’ Concern
en_content: So far in his tenure, the president has given far fewer one-on-one interviews than his two predecessors. Some Democrats are asking if he could be making better use of his White House pulpit.

title: As Inflation Surges, Pakistan Seeks a $6 Billion I.M.F. Lifeline

en_content: Rising prices and a weakened currency are straining households, intensifying pressure on Prime Minister Imran Khan to find solutions.

title: Revitalizing Black Neighborhoods by Preserving Their History
content: Developers are repurposing deteriorated structures in historically significant areas in an effort to incorporate spaces for the arts, affordable housing and small businesses under one roof.

title: Jeff Bezos gives $100 million to the Obama Foundation.

title: Peter Buck, Co-Founder of the Subway Sandwich Chain, Dies at 90
content: The $1,000 loan he gave to a friend’s son was used to start a single sandwich shop. That shop grew into the world’s biggest fast-food chain.

en_content: The $1,000 loan he gave to a friend’s son was used to start a single sandwich shop. That shop grew into the world’s biggest fast-food chain.

title: CVS, Walgreens and Walmart Fueled Opioid Crisis, Jury Finds
content: This is the first jury verdict in an opioid case. The decision embraced a key legal argument that judges in other opioid cases had recently rejected.
